# UC-18.3.10 ┬Ę EVPN Route Type Distribution and Control-Plane Growth Signatures

## Description

Tracks **BGP EVPN** **NLRI** inventory by **RFC 7432** route familyŌĆö**Type-2 MAC/IP**, **Type-3 inclusive multicast Ethernet tag (IMET)**, **Type-5 IP prefix**, and residual **Other**ŌĆöacross **VXLAN** fabrics built on **Cisco NX-OS**, **Arista EOS**, or **Juniper Junos** leaves. Rises in **Type-2** usually follow workload mobility or silent duplication; **Type-3** shifts trace **BUM** scope or **multi-homing** changes; **Type-5** jumps often mean **inter-VRF** **L3** handoffs, **WAN** **leaks**, or **centralized routing** redeploys. Splunk compares normalized counters from structured polls plus optional **syslog** hints and **BGP neighbor** posture so operators separate real **control-plane** pressure from transient **rebalance** windows.

## Value

Route-type mix is a **capacity** language **network** leadership can read: **Type-2** dominance funds tenant-isolation decisions, **Type-5** share spikes justify **prefix** summarization or **import** **policy** fixes before **RIB** memory exhausts, and **Type-3** excursions highlight **flooding** or **core-multicast** work ahead of **storm** complaints. Trending the mix buys quarters of runway for **line-card** planning, **refresh** waves, and **automation** guardrailsŌĆöwithout waiting for **hardware** **alerts** or **TAC** **sev1** bridges.

## Implementation

Land **NX-API**/**eAPI** JSON on **`nxos:bgp_evpn`** and **`arista:evpn_summary`** via **1546**; normalize multi-vendor Ansible output into **`network:evpn_routes`**; mirror **`network:bgp_neighbor`** **l2vpn evpn** state; optionally enrich with **`cisco:nxos:syslog`**. **FIELDALIAS** vendor fields (`vrf`, `route_total`, `evpn_route_type`). Schedule **`evpn_route_type_distribution_core`** every fifteen minutes; maintain **`fabric_site_pod_map`** and **`evpn_route_thresholds`** lookups for alert math.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
**Splunk REST Modular Input (1546)** belongs on hardened Heavy Forwarders that terminate TLS to NX-API, eAPI, or an automation gatewayŌĆönever on open laptops. Store vault references in `passwords.conf`, keep `inputs.conf` free of clear-text secrets, and document the poll account in the same CMDB row as the device. Route all EVPN summary events into **`dc_fabric`** or **`network_ops`** so **`nxos:bgp_evpn`** and **`network:bgp_neighbor`** can be joined without cross-index hops that confuse junior on-call engineers. Publish **`fabric_site_pod_map.csv`** with leaf hostname, pod_label, region_code, and optional **maintenance_owner** columns; pair it with **`evpn_route_thresholds.csv`** that lists per-site **warn_routes** and **crit_routes** for each **`rt_bucket`**. Assign least-privilege **`fabric_l2`** roles that may search those indexes but cannot edit authentication lists. When Type-2 bursts need correlation with hypervisor motion, enable **Splunk Add-on for VMware (1810)** task feeds; when NSX-T policy drives VNI churn, add **Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX (4856)** inventory snapshots. Executive buyers appreciate **Cisco Data Center Networking App (7777)** panels once Cisco sourcetypes land; reserve **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI (4022)** syslog only for hybrid sites and keep APIC-sourced events out of pure VXLAN BGP totals.  Capture the exact NX-OS, EOS, or Junos software versions in each event either as a KV pair from automation or as a nightly inventory join; splices between code trains often change human-readable strings while counters stay semantically identical. For regulated environments, annotate whether polling accounts use read-only RBAC on devices and rotate those passwords with the same rigor as interactive admin accounts. Finally, capture written baselines for the normal percentage of Type-5 routes per pod so future reviewers know whether a spike is novel. Document firewall permits from the Heavy Forwarder subnet to each polled loopback, and keep a one-page RACI that states who updates route-type thresholds after a hardware refresh. Brownfield merges should restart baseline windows for at least two weeks so predecessor-fabric history does not skew growth detection.

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
Schedule **`show bgp l2vpn evpn summary`** via NX-API **ins_api** POSTs and map the JSON tables into **`sourcetype=nxos:bgp_evpn`** with numeric **`route_total`**, textual **`evpn_route_type`**, **`vrf`**, **vni**, and authoritative **host** fields. For Arista, issue **`show bgp evpn summary`** through eAPI **execute** calls landing on **`sourcetype=arista:evpn_summary`**, retaining VLAN-aware bundle identifiers when the CLI splits counters per instance. If Ansible or Terraform already emits per-type totals, normalize MAC/IP, IMET, and prefix columns into **`network:evpn_routes`** JSON so automation metadata rides alongside metrics. Stand up **`network:bgp_neighbor`** polls filtered to the **l2vpn evpn** address family, capturing **neighbor** IP, **state**, **uptime**, prefixes received, and last error text. Use **`cisco:nxos:syslog`** only to capture **L2RIB** or **EVPN** threshold traps that lack structured JSONŌĆönever as the primary counter. Keep polling between **five and fifteen minutes**, slower during ISSU rehearsals, and log REST HTTP status codes alongside each payload for vendor support. Expect roughly two to six kilobytes per leaf per poll plus transient doubles during image upgrades; size license impact before turning on five-minute cadences across thousands of devices.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search
Import the saved SPL from this JSON file, wrap the opening index union inside macro **`%evpn_route_indexes%`**, and save **`evpn_route_type_distribution_core`** as a report without acceleration until validation completes. Clone **`evpn_route_type_growth_alert`** by appending **`| where growth_flag="hot_growth"`** plus a **lookup** join to **`evpn_route_thresholds`** so alerting thresholds stay data-driven. Mirror the same SPL text inside **`savedsearches.conf`** descriptions to prevent silent UI edits from drifting away from Git. Where DevOps requires ephemeral stacks, add a suffix token to macro values rather than editing the saved search body. Capture version tags in Git whenever REST field names change so rollback is boring instead of heroic.

Understanding this SPL
The **OR** index stanza reflects how network operations teams actually name indexes; rename to your standard but keep it synchronized with data onboarding tickets. **`rt_bucket`** translates vendor-specific spellings into stable **RT2_MAC_IP**, **RT3_IMET**, **RT5_IP_PREFIX**, and **RT_OTHER** labels so dashboard builders need not fork charts per OEM. **`tonumber(route_total)`** drops warm-boot **N/A** tokens before **`timechart`** runs, avoiding fake cliffs. **`streamstats`** **`routes_prev`** highlights fresh deltas that matter more than a leaf that has been loud but flat for hours. **`leaf_p95`** creates an adaptive gate for young fleets without multi-year baselines. **`lookup fabric_site_pod_map`** decorates rows with pod_label and region_code for leadership overlays. **`capacity_hint`** gives a secondary color cue when absolute scaleŌĆönot just growthŌĆöenters a vendor-documented uneasy band. **`pct_of_leaf_p95`** surfaces ratio context on ad-hoc panels without altering the main alert math.

Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
On a maintenance-approved NX-OS leaf, compare **`show bgp l2vpn evpn summary`** route-type columns with Splunk **`latest(route_total)`**; accept up to **3%** drift when polling lags a few seconds behind manual CLI. EOS validation uses **`show bgp evpn summary`** paired with **`arista:evpn_summary`**. For Junos fabrics, align **`show evpn overview`** counters with **`network:evpn_routes`** until field names stabilize after code upgrades. Ensure only **one structured event per leaf per 15m bin**ŌĆömultiple hits indicate duplicate REST inputs. Border leaves should lead **`RT5_IP_PREFIX`** counts; if pure access switches suddenly dominate Type-5, suspect a mis-scoped collector pulling an entire VPN table. When **`network:bgp_neighbor`** shows **Idle** peers while totals climb, pause paging until REST authentication or CPU starvation on the switch is ruled out. Archive validation screenshots in the change record so future auditors can replay the same checks.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Dashboard row one should be a stacked area chart summing **`routes`** by **`rt_bucket`** with a **time picker** default of seven days. Row two: a sortable table of the top twenty leaves by **`routes_peak`** with **`vrf_fanout`**, **`vrf_spread_band`**, and **`capacity_hint`**. Row three: a scatter plot of **`routes` vs `leaf_p95`** that encodes **`growth_flag`** using icon shape. Schedule weekly reviews of **`RT_OTHER`** proportion and export the saved search monthly for capacity leadership decks. DRI mapping: L3 architects own Type-5 drift, L2 switching leads own Type-2 pressure, multicast engineers own Type-3-only anomalies, and automation teams own duplicate-ingest defects. Add a short drill video for NOC staff that shows how to pivot from the dashboard into raw events without running ad-hoc wide-open searches.

Step 5 ŌĆö Operationalize & Troubleshoot
Visualization: adopt fixed hex colors (**RT2_MAC_IP `#2ca02c`**, **RT3_IMET `#ff7f0e`**, **RT5_IP_PREFIX `#1f77b4`**, **RT_OTHER `#7f7f7f`**) and enable brush-linked drilldowns that open the underlying **`leaf`** events with field sidebars pinned for **VNI** and **VRF**. Alert design: run every **thirty minutes** on **`earliest=-4h@m`**; trigger when **`growth_flag=hot_growth`**, **`routes` exceeds warn_routes**, and **`alert_seed=1`**; throttle **one hundred twenty minutes** per **leaf plus rt_bucket**; escalate severity when **`routes` surpasses crit_routes** or **`delta_routes`** is twenty percent above the trailing four-hour average. Payloads must include **`fabric_site`**, **`pod_label`**, **`region_code`**, **`rt_bucket`**, **`routes`**, **`routes_peak`**, **`delta_routes`**, **`capacity_hint`**, plus the latest **Established** state from **`network:bgp_neighbor`**ŌĆönever embed API keys. Troubleshooting cues: all families at zero means REST failures; **RT5-only** surges map to WAN or import-map changes; **RT2-only** bursts require mobility or CNI investigations per **UC-18.3.5**; **RT3-only** climbs point to BUM or ingress-replication edits; RT_OTHER above fifteen percent for a day signals **`props`** drift, not customer demand.  When a data center refresh adds new line cards, revalidate thresholds because ASIC profiles change what ŌĆ£comfortableŌĆØ route counts mean. If legal requires data residency, confirm Heavy Forwarders parsing REST payloads stay in the approved geography even when switches sit abroad. During blackouts, operators should still be able to open the saved report from a cold cache copy exported nightly to object storage. If charts look healthy yet applications fail, pivot to **UC-18.3.4** for tunnel health before declaring this use case clean.

## SPL

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network)
(
  sourcetype="nxos:bgp_evpn" OR
  sourcetype="arista:evpn_summary" OR
  sourcetype="network:evpn_routes" OR
  sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR
  sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor"
)
earliest=-24h@m
| eval leaf=trim(coalesce(host,device_name,switch_name,nms_hostname,""))
| eval vrf_key=trim(coalesce(vrf,vrf_name,l3vni_name,"default"))
| eval route_type_raw=coalesce(evpn_route_type,nlri_type,route_type,bgp_evpn_rt,cli_rt_label,type,"")
| eval rt_bucket=case(
    match(lower(route_type_raw),"(?i)mac.?ip|type-?2|^2$|rt-?2|l2vpn.*2"),"RT2_MAC_IP",
    match(lower(route_type_raw),"(?i)imet|inclusive.?multicast|type-?3|^3$|rt-?3"),"RT3_IMET",
    match(lower(route_type_raw),"(?i)ip.?prefix|type-?5|^5$|rt-?5|prefix"),"RT5_IP_PREFIX",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)route-type:2|mac/ip"),"RT2_MAC_IP",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)route-type:3|imet"),"RT3_IMET",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)route-type:5|ip prefix"),"RT5_IP_PREFIX",
    true(),"RT_OTHER")
| eval route_total=tonumber(coalesce(route_total,nlri_count,evpn_paths,prefix_count,num_routes,route_entries,evpn_route_count,count,""))
| where isnotnull(route_total) AND route_total>=0 AND isnotnull(leaf) AND leaf!=""
| bin _time span=15m
| stats latest(route_total) AS routes max(route_total) AS routes_peak dc(vrf_key) AS vrf_fanout
    values(route_type_raw) AS rt_hints BY _time leaf rt_bucket
| sort 0 leaf rt_bucket _time
| streamstats window=2 global=f current=f last(routes) AS routes_prev BY leaf rt_bucket
| eval delta_routes=if(isnotnull(routes_prev),routes-routes_prev,0)
| eventstats perc95(routes) AS leaf_p95 BY leaf
| eval growth_flag=if(routes>=(leaf_p95*1.25) AND leaf_p95>0,"hot_growth","steady")
| eval pct_of_leaf_p95=if(leaf_p95>0,round(100*routes/leaf_p95,1),null())
| eval fabric_site=trim(coalesce(pod_id,site_code,fabric_token,fabric_site,"unspecified"))
| lookup fabric_site_pod_map leaf OUTPUTNEW pod_label region_code
| eval vrf_spread_band=case(vrf_fanout<=6,"low",vrf_fanout<=18,"medium",true(),"high")
| eval capacity_hint=case(
    rt_bucket=="RT2_MAC_IP" AND routes>200000,"rt2_headroom_watch",
    rt_bucket=="RT5_IP_PREFIX" AND routes>80000,"rt5_headroom_watch",
    true(),"nominal")
| eval alert_seed=if(growth_flag=="hot_growth" AND delta_routes>0 AND routes_peak>0,1,0)
| eval poll_quality=if(isnotnull(routes_peak) AND routes_peak>=routes,"complete","review")
| eval diag_key=leaf."|".rt_bucket
| timechart span=1h sum(routes) AS evpn_routes by rt_bucket
```

## Visualization

(1) **Stacked area timechart** ŌĆö **sum(routes)** by **`rt_bucket`** per **`fabric_site`**. (2) **100% normalized column** ŌĆö rolling **share** of **`RT5_IP_PREFIX`** vs **RT2**/**RT3**. (3) **Table** ŌĆö **top leaves** by **`routes_peak`** with **`vrf_fanout`** and **`growth_flag`**. (4) **Scatter** ŌĆö **`routes`** vs **`leaf_p95`** highlighting **`hot_growth`**. (5) **Single value** + sparkline ŌĆö **total EVPN routes** fleet-wide with week-over-week delta.

## Known False Positives

**rr_readvert_wave** ŌĆö Spinning route-reflector clusters or collapsing RR partitions forces every leaf to re-readvertise IMET and MAC/IP routes even though dataplane forwarding stayed warm; Splunk rows cluster on MAINT ticketsŌĆösuppress until **`network:bgp_neighbor`** shows Established uptime climbing while withdraw counters remain flat.

**imet_scope_expansion** ŌĆö Operations widen the BUM domain or add multi-site segments, so Type-3 totals step once while Type-2 barely moves; treat as noise when design docs cite PIM-SSM or ingress-replication edits in the same hour and Type-5 share is unchanged.

**collector_field_rename_skew** ŌĆö A minor TA upgrade renames **`evpn_route_type`** to **`nlri_family`** without updating inputs; RT_OTHER balloons while CLI still looks fineŌĆöfix props before paging capacity.

**l3vpn_import_shuffle** ŌĆö VRF import map refactors replay Type-5 prefixes while MAC tables sleep; correlate peaks on RT5_IP_PREFIX with vrf_key lists that match WAN team change IDs, not server sprawl.

**telemetry_duplicate_streams** ŌĆö Accidentally ingesting both **`nxos:bgp_evpn`** and a legacy **`network:evpn_routes`** stream for the same leaf doubles counts until dedupŌĆövalidate dc(leaf) per poll window equals one before trusting growth signals.

**graceful_restart_stale_echo** ŌĆö RFC 4724 helper modes keep STALE paths visible; route totals may read high versus what the RIB actually usesŌĆöcross-check with **`show bgp l2vpn evpn route`** output before buying hardware.

**centralized_irb_cutover** ŌĆö Moving default-gateway anycast IRB between designs inflates Type-2 replays without new VMsŌĆömatch Splunk timestamps to symmetric_irb CAB tokens before blaming tenants.

**synthetic_lab_bgp_reset** ŌĆö Certification labs repeatedly clear BGP neighbors to teach failover behavior; route-type totals oscillate wildly without production impactŌĆöcross-check lab device names against a lab_allow.csv lookup before paging.
